nodes.label: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior
nodes.norm_label: agents.md — orion subagent development contract: repo-wide rules for how ai coding agents work in orion-sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Prime directive: do not build cathedrals — prefer thin seams, small patches, explicit contracts, fast tests, and visible evidence; design mode gets a design artifact, implementation mode gets a finished branch with tests/evals/docs/review/PR report
nodes.norm_label: prime directive: do not build cathedrals — prefer thin seams, small patches, explicit contracts, fast tests, and visible evidence; design mode gets a design artifact, implementation mode gets a finished branch with tests/evals/docs/review/pr report
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: No keyword cathedrals: labels/enums/taxonomies/ontology nodes without a schema contract, producer, consumer, reducer, UI surface, metric, test, eval, or live smoke attached in the same patch are banned as junk that names the world without changing runtime behavior
nodes.norm_label: no keyword cathedrals: labels/enums/taxonomies/ontology nodes without a schema contract, producer, consumer, reducer, ui surface, metric, test, eval, or live smoke attached in the same patch are banned as junk that names the world without changing runtime behavior
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Event substrate first: grow from events -> schema -> trace -> reducer -> projection -> eval -> UI, not from a hand-authored mind-palace ontology with no runtime proof
nodes.norm_label: event substrate first: grow from events -> schema -> trace -> reducer -> projection -> eval -> ui, not from a hand-authored mind-palace ontology with no runtime proof
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Runtime truth beats config truth: config being set or code existing is not proof; runtime claims need evidence from the live rail (health response, emitted/consumed event, stored artifact, reducer cursor movement, projection, log line, test output) or must be marked UNVERIFIED
nodes.norm_label: runtime truth beats config truth: config being set or code existing is not proof; runtime claims need evidence from the live rail (health response, emitted/consumed event, stored artifact, reducer cursor movement, projection, log line, test output) or must be marked unverified
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: No empty-shell cognition: do not ship cognition-shaped output with no substance — empty semantic projections, placeholder memory cards, fallback text as generated cognition, raw_len=0 treated as success, stale reducer cursors are all invalid success states requiring inspectable evidence
nodes.norm_label: no empty-shell cognition: do not ship cognition-shaped output with no substance — empty semantic projections, placeholder memory cards, fallback text as generated cognition, raw_len=0 treated as success, stale reducer cursors are all invalid success states requiring inspectable evidence
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Deterministic gates over repeated yelling: if Juniper has to repeat a rule twice, turn it into a script/test/check/hook/make target instead of a louder prompt
nodes.norm_label: deterministic gates over repeated yelling: if juniper has to repeat a rule twice, turn it into a script/test/check/hook/make target instead of a louder prompt
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Thin seams, not ornamental layers: every new layer must isolate failure, expose state, simplify a caller, create a typed contract, or make a test possible; do not add a service/registry/planner unless it removes ambiguity
nodes.norm_label: thin seams, not ornamental layers: every new layer must isolate failure, expose state, simplify a caller, create a typed contract, or make a test possible; do not add a service/registry/planner unless it removes ambiguity
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Proposal mode required before invasive cognition changes: memory, identity, self-modeling, autonomy, private recall, social continuity, or cognition-loop changes need a proposal naming capability change, data touched, privacy boundary, proof trace, dangerous failure mode, and rollback before implementation
nodes.norm_label: proposal mode required before invasive cognition changes: memory, identity, self-modeling, autonomy, private recall, social continuity, or cognition-loop changes need a proposal naming capability change, data touched, privacy boundary, proof trace, dangerous failure mode, and rollback before implementation
nodes.community_name: Memory Constellation (tri-layer SQL/RDF/vector)

nodes.label: Privacy and blocked material stay blocked: raw private traces, journals, mirrors, and internal memory artifacts must not leak through convenience surfaces; summaries/projections preserve privacy boundaries
nodes.norm_label: privacy and blocked material stay blocked: raw private traces, journals, mirrors, and internal memory artifacts must not leak through convenience surfaces; summaries/projections preserve privacy boundaries
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: No regex swamp: regex is a narrow sensor/parser/legacy fallback, not a cognition architecture; extensible behavior splits into detectors, signals, scoring, policy, and assembly
nodes.norm_label: no regex swamp: regex is a narrow sensor/parser/legacy fallback, not a cognition architecture; extensible behavior splits into detectors, signals, scoring, policy, and assembly
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Follow-through is part of the feature: a patch isn't done until the branch is clean, tests/evals pass, review is fixed, env is synced, Docker checks run, restart commands are listed, the branch is pushed, and the PR report exists
nodes.norm_label: follow-through is part of the feature: a patch isn't done until the branch is clean, tests/evals pass, review is fixed, env is synced, docker checks run, restart commands are listed, the branch is pushed, and the pr report exists
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Clean git and worktree rules (§2): start with git status/branch check, classify dirt before mixing, prefer a separate worktree for parallel subagent work — stated as prose policy, not an enforced mechanism
nodes.norm_label: clean git and worktree rules (§2): start with git status/branch check, classify dirt before mixing, prefer a separate worktree for parallel subagent work — stated as prose policy, not an enforced mechanism
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Docker readiness (§8): run docker compose builds/deploys through scripts/safe_docker_build.sh instead of calling docker compose directly; it refuses to run from the shared/primary checkout and applies the --env-file/-f pattern automatically; raw docker compose examples retained only as reference for one-off logs/ps commands
nodes.norm_label: docker readiness (§8): run docker compose builds/deploys through scripts/safe_docker_build.sh instead of calling docker compose directly; it refuses to run from the shared/primary checkout and applies the --env-file/-f pattern automatically; raw docker compose examples retained only as reference for one-off logs/ps commands
nodes.community_name: reviews/pending/2026-07-14-agent-worktree-discipline-and-graphify-sync-spec.md — DRAFT design spec bundling two independent problems that share infrastructure: enforcing worktree-only writes at the git level, and closing a graphify sync gap so the knowledge graph doesn't drift from main

nodes.label: Env/config/settings contract (§7): env parity is non-negotiable — any add/remove/rename of an env key must update .env_example, local .env, settings.py, docker-compose.yml, requirements.txt, and README in the same changeset, via scripts/sync_local_env_from_example.py
nodes.norm_label: env/config/settings contract (§7): env parity is non-negotiable — any add/remove/rename of an env key must update .env_example, local .env, settings.py, docker-compose.yml, requirements.txt, and readme in the same changeset, via scripts/sync_local_env_from_example.py
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Review gate (§12): before handoff, run tests, run evals or explain the gap, run the code review skill in a subagent, fix all material findings, re-run affected checks; unresolved material issues force DONE_WITH_CONCERNS or BLOCKED
nodes.norm_label: review gate (§12): before handoff, run tests, run evals or explain the gap, run the code review skill in a subagent, fix all material findings, re-run affected checks; unresolved material issues force done_with_concerns or blocked
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Safety rules (§13): never rm -rf, git reset --hard, git push --force, DROP TABLE, TRUNCATE, kubectl delete, docker volume rm, or production writes without explicit approval; never git commit/push --no-verify
nodes.norm_label: safety rules (§13): never rm -rf, git reset --hard, git push --force, drop table, truncate, kubectl delete, docker volume rm, or production writes without explicit approval; never git commit/push --no-verify
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: PR description template (§18): required Markdown shape covering summary, outcome moved, architecture touched, files changed, schema/bus/API changes, env/config changes, tests/evals/docker checks run, review findings fixed, restart required, risks
nodes.norm_label: pr description template (§18): required markdown shape covering summary, outcome moved, architecture touched, files changed, schema/bus/api changes, env/config changes, tests/evals/docker checks run, review findings fixed, restart required, risks
nodes.community_name: AGENTS.md — Orion Subagent Development Contract: repo-wide rules for how AI coding agents work in Orion-Sapienform, aimed at inspectable, testable, parallel-safe agent behavior

nodes.label: Completion status (§19): every task ends in exactly one of DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, BLOCKED, or NEEDS_CONTEXT — never 'partially done'
nodes.norm_label: completion status (§19): every task ends in exactly one of done, done_with_concerns, blocked, or needs_context — never 'partially done'

nodes.label: scripts/safe_docker_build.sh — required wrapper for all docker compose build/up/deploy in this repo, replacing bare docker compose calls; refuses to run from the shared/primary checkout (worktrees only) and applies the standard --env-file/-f pattern automatically

nodes.community_name: reviews/pending/2026-07-14-agent-worktree-discipline-and-graphify-sync-spec.md — DRAFT design spec bundling two independent problems that share infrastructure: enforcing worktree-only writes at the git level, and closing a graphify sync gap so the knowledge graph doesn't drift from main

nodes.label: Incident: a concurrent agent session ran docker compose build+up straight from the shared/primary checkout and silently reverted another session's already-verified fix — the concrete motivation for both the safe_docker_build.sh wrapper and the pre-commit shared-checkout guard
nodes.norm_label: incident: a concurrent agent session ran docker compose build+up straight from the shared/primary checkout and silently reverted another session's already-verified fix — the concrete motivation for both the safe_docker_build.sh wrapper and the pre-commit shared-checkout guard
